questions.question: What is the largest planet in our Solar System?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the largest of all the planets in terms of both mass and size.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: The soil on Mars appears reddish in color, which gives it its nickname.

questions.question: What is the Moon's diameter compared to Earth's?
questions.correct_answer: About a quarter of Earth's diameter
questions.explanation: The Moon is about one-fourth the size of the Earth in diameter.

questions.question: How many moons does Mars have?
questions.explanation: Mars has two natural satellites, Phobos and Deimos.

questions.question: What is Pluto's status according to the International Astronomical Union (IAU)?
questions.explanation: Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006 by the IAU.

questions.question: Which of the following planets can be seen with the naked eye?
questions.correct_answer: Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, and Saturn
questions.explanation: These five planets are visible to the naked eye from Earth.

questions.question: How long does it take the Moon to complete one revolution around the Earth?
questions.explanation: The Moon orbits the Earth in approximately 27.3 days.

questions.question: What is the largest group of objects that orbit around a planet called?
questions.explanation: A satellite system consists of multiple satellites orbiting a single planet.

questions.question: Which planet has the most moons in our Solar System?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is orbited by a total of 92 confirmed moons.

questions.question: What is the term for objects that are smaller than planets but still orbit around them?
questions.explanation: Smaller bodies like dwarf planets, asteroids, and comets can also be found in our Solar System.

important_notes.title: The Sun is a star
important_notes.content: The Sun is the center of our Solar System and provides light and heat to Earth.

important_notes.title: The planets are divided into two main groups: rocky and gas giants
important_notes.content: Rocky planets like Earth have solid surfaces, while gas giants like Jupiter are primarily composed of gases.

important_notes.title: Asteroids and comets are smaller than planets but still orbit around the Sun
important_notes.content: These objects can be found in our Solar System and often have unique orbits.

important_notes.title: The Moon is Earth's natural satellite
important_notes.content: The Moon orbits the Earth in approximately 27.3 days.

important_notes.title: Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006
important_notes.content: This decision was made by the International Astronomical Union (IAU).

facts.fact: The Solar System is estimated to be around 4.6 billion years old.
Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun, with an average distance of about 58 million kilometers.
Venus is often called the hottest planet due to its thick atmosphere and surface temperature reaching up to 462°C.
Mars has the largest volcano in our Solar System, Olympus Mons, which stands at a height of over 27 km.
Jupiter's Great Red Spot is a persistent anticyclonic storm that has been raging for centuries.
